name: SHAPE elamipretide healthy-aging pilot
summary: A recruiting open-label, single-arm phase 2a pilot testing four weeks of daily subcutaneous elamipretide for safety, tolerability, inflammation markers, mobility, cognition, and skeletal-muscle strength in adults aged 65 to 80.
population: Adults aged 65 to 80 with lower function and VO2peak thresholds used as evidence of lower mitochondrial function

trial_details.expected_results_window: The registry record lists Apr. 15, 2026 as the study end date; no results were posted in the Jun. 2, 2026 record version.
trial_details.horizon_note: Near-term timing makes this worth watching, but a small open-label pilot would still need cautious interpretation.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Healthy-aging elamipretide data could connect mitochondrial intervention to mobility, cognition, strength, safety, and inflammation readouts in older adults.
